entities.name: ServiceAppointment
entities.label: Service Appointment
entities.description: The core record for every roadside assistance call. Each time a member calls AAA for help, a Service Appointment (SA) is created. It tracks the full lifecycle: creation, dispatch, driver arrival, job completion, and any cancellations or declines. This is the primary object for all metrics.

entities.name: ServiceTerritory
entities.label: Service Territory (Garage)
entities.description: Represents a garage or service territory — the physical facility where drivers are based. Each garage has assigned drivers, zone coverage, and a facility account. Garages are ranked by composite score and graded A–F.

entities.name: WorkType
entities.label: Work Type
entities.description: Defines the type of roadside service: Tow, Battery, Tire, Lockout, Locksmith, Winch Out, Fuel/Miscellaneous, PVS, Jumpstart, or Tow Drop-Off. Determines which driver skills are required and affects metric calculations (Tow Drop-Off is excluded from counts).

entities.name: WorkOrder
entities.label: Work Order
entities.description: Parent record that groups one or more Service Appointments together. The Work Order Number is the key used to link customer satisfaction surveys back to specific calls. Each SA belongs to one Work Order.

entities.name: ServiceResource
entities.label: Service Resource (Driver/Truck)
entities.description: Represents a driver or truck in the fleet. For internal fleet: this is the actual driver with their GPS location from the FSL mobile app. For Towbook contractors: may represent a truck identifier. Tracks real-time location, skills, and driver type.

entities.name: ServiceTerritoryMember
entities.label: Territory Membership
entities.description: Junction object linking a driver (ServiceResource) to a garage (ServiceTerritory). A driver can belong to multiple territories as primary or secondary. Used to count fleet size per garage and determine which drivers can be assigned to calls in that territory.

entities.name: Skill
entities.label: Skill
entities.description: Defines a capability that drivers can have: Tow, Flat Bed, Battery, Tire, Lockout, etc. Skills determine which call types a driver is qualified to handle. Used in driver recommendation and cascade logic.

entities.name: ServiceResourceSkill
entities.label: Driver Skill Assignment
entities.description: Junction object linking a driver (ServiceResource) to a skill (Skill). Each row means 'this driver can perform this type of service.' Used to classify drivers as tow-capable, light-service-capable, or battery-capable for dispatch matching.

entities.name: AssignedResource
entities.label: Assigned Resource
entities.description: Junction object that records which driver was assigned to which Service Appointment. Created when a driver is dispatched to a call. Used to build the Driver Leaderboard — linking individual driver performance to specific calls and timing data.

entities.name: ServiceAppointmentHistory
entities.label: SA History (Audit Trail)
entities.description: Standard Salesforce field history tracking for Service Appointments. Records every field change with old value, new value, and timestamp. Critical for two purposes: (1) determining 1st Call vs 2nd+ Call by tracking territory assignment sequence, (2) capturing real Towbook arrival times via 'On Location' status transitions.

entities.name: ERS_Territory_Priority_Matrix__c
entities.label: Territory Priority Matrix
entities.description: Custom object defining the zone cascade chain — which garages serve which zones, in what priority order, and for which work types. When a call comes in from a zone, the system dispatches to the Priority 1 garage first. If that garage declines, it cascades to Priority 2, then 3, etc.

entities.name: Survey_Result__c
entities.label: Customer Satisfaction Survey
entities.description: Custom object storing post-service customer surveys from Qualtrics. Sent to members after their call is completed. Matched back to the garage via Work Order Number. Surveys can arrive days after the call, so recent periods may have incomplete survey data.

entities.name: Account
entities.label: Facility Account
entities.description: Standard Salesforce Account record representing the garage's business entity. Linked to ServiceTerritory via ERS_Facility_Account__r. Provides the garage phone number and account-level dispatch method classification.

entities.name: FSL__Polygon__c
entities.label: Territory Polygon (Boundary)
entities.description: Managed package object storing the geographic boundary of each territory as KML data. Each polygon defines the physical area a garage covers on the map. Used to render territory boundaries on the Command Center map layer.

entities.name: FSL__Scheduling_Policy__c
entities.label: Scheduling Policy
entities.description: Managed package object defining the rules and goals used by the FSL optimization engine when auto-dispatching calls. Includes work rules (constraints) and service goals (optimization objectives) that determine how calls are assigned to drivers.

entities.name: SkillRequirement
entities.label: Skill Requirement
entities.description: Junction object linking a WorkType to the Skills required to perform it. For example, a 'Tow' work type requires the 'Tow' skill and optionally 'Flat Bed.' Used by the dispatch engine and simulator to determine which drivers are qualified for a given call type.

entities.name: Asset
entities.label: Asset (ERS Truck)
entities.description: Represents a physical truck in the fleet. Filtered by RecordType 'ERS Truck' to get only roadside assistance vehicles. Links to a driver via ERS_Driver__c and carries truck capabilities (tow, flatbed, light service). Used to match truck capabilities to call requirements in dispatch recommendations and the Command Center map.

entities.name: Shift
entities.label: Driver Shift
entities.description: Records a driver's scheduled work shift — start time, end time, assigned territory, and status. Used to determine driver availability for dispatch recommendations and capacity planning. A driver is only considered available if they have an active shift covering the current time window.

entities.name: ResourceAbsence
entities.label: Resource Absence
entities.description: Records when a driver is unavailable — vacation, sick leave, training, truck maintenance, etc. An absence overlapping the current time means the driver cannot be dispatched. Used alongside Shift records to build the complete picture of driver availability.

entities.name: ERS_Service_Appointment_PTA__c
entities.label: PTA Configuration
entities.description: Custom object storing the configured PTA (Promised Time of Arrival) values per territory and work type. Defines how many minutes should be promised to the member based on the garage and service type. Used by the PTA Advisor page to compare configured PTA targets against actual performance.

entities.name: FSL__Scheduling_Policy_Goal__c
entities.label: Scheduling Policy Goal
entities.description: Junction object linking a Scheduling Policy to a Service Goal with a weight. Each policy can have multiple optimization goals (e.g., minimize travel time, maximize resource utilization) with different weights that control how the FSL engine prioritizes trade-offs during auto-dispatch.

entities.name: FSL__Service_Goal__c
entities.label: Service Goal
entities.description: Defines an optimization objective for the FSL scheduling engine — e.g., 'Minimize Travel', 'Maximize Resource Utilization', 'Prioritize By Priority.' Referenced by Scheduling Policy Goals with assigned weights.

entities.name: FSL__Scheduling_Policy_Work_Rule__c
entities.label: Scheduling Policy Work Rule
entities.description: Junction object linking a Scheduling Policy to a Work Rule. Defines which constraints (work rules) apply under each scheduling policy.

entities.name: FSL__Work_Rule__c
entities.label: Work Rule
entities.description: Defines a constraint for the FSL scheduling engine — e.g., 'Match Skills', 'Match Territory', 'Working Hours', 'Maximum Travel Distance.' Work rules prevent invalid assignments (a driver without tow capability won't be assigned a tow call).

fields.label: Record ID
fields.description: Unique Salesforce record ID for the Service Appointment. This is the primary key used in all joins, lookups, and API references throughout the application.

fields.label: Appointment Number
fields.description: Human-readable appointment number (e.g., SA-00012345). This is the number displayed to users in the SA lookup, queue board, and detail views. Auto-generated by Salesforce when the record is created.

fields.label: Call Created Time
fields.description: The exact moment AAA received the member's roadside assistance call and created the Service Appointment in Salesforce. This is when the clock starts ticking for ALL response time calculations. Every metric that measures 'how long the member waited' starts from this timestamp. ATA (Actual Time of Arrival) = ActualStartTime minus CreatedDate.

fields.label: SA Status
fields.description: Current lifecycle status of the service call. Possible values: 'Dispatched' (driver assigned, en route to member), 'Assigned' (waiting for a driver), 'Completed' (job finished successfully), 'Canceled' (call cancelled before service), 'Cancel Call - Service Not En Route' (cancelled before driver departed), 'Cancel Call - Service En Route' (cancelled after driver departed), 'Unable to Complete' (driver arrived but couldn't finish), 'No-Show' (member not at location). Status drives completion rate calculations and open call counts.

fields.label: Assigned Garage (Territory)
fields.description: The garage/territory currently assigned to handle this call. This determines which garage's metrics are affected by this call. When a call is dispatched, it is assigned to a specific garage. If that garage declines and the call cascades to another garage, this field is updated to the new garage.

fields.label: Work Type Reference
fields.description: Links this Service Appointment to the type of service needed (Tow, Battery, Tire, Lockout, etc.). The work type determines which drivers are qualified to handle the call and affects cascade logic. 'Tow Drop-Off' work type triggers automatic exclusion from all response time and count metrics.

fields.label: Scheduled Start (Dispatch Time)
fields.description: When a driver was scheduled or assigned to respond to this call. Set by the FSL optimization engine during auto-dispatch, or manually by a dispatcher. Used to decompose the member's total wait time: 'Dispatch Queue' = time from CreatedDate to SchedStartTime (how long the call sat before someone was assigned). 'Travel Time' = SchedStartTime to ActualStartTime (how long the driver took to reach the member).

fields.label: Scheduled End Time
fields.description: Projected end time for the service call, calculated by adding the estimated duration to SchedStartTime. Used by the scheduling engine for capacity planning but not directly shown in app metrics.

fields.label: Driver Arrival Time
fields.description: The moment the driver physically arrived at the member's location. For Fleet drivers: set when the driver taps 'Arrived' in the FSL mobile app — this is the real, precise arrival time. For Towbook contractors: written by the 'Integrations Towbook' system user at completion as a future-projected estimate, NOT the real arrival. For Towbook, the actual arrival is captured in ServiceAppointmentHistory when Status changes to 'On Location'. This is the END of the clock for response time: ATA = ActualStartTime minus CreatedDate.

fields.label: Job Completion Time
fields.description: When the driver finished the roadside service and marked the Service Appointment as complete. Used to calculate on-site duration (how long the driver spent helping the member): On-Site Duration = ActualEndTime minus ActualStartTime. Also used for total call duration: ActualEndTime minus CreatedDate.

fields.label: Member Location (Latitude)
fields.description: GPS latitude of the member's stranded location — where they are waiting for roadside help. Used for map display (pin on the Command Center map) and distance calculations when recommending the nearest available driver.

fields.label: Member Location (Longitude)
fields.description: GPS longitude of the member's stranded location. Works with Latitude to pinpoint the exact location on the map and calculate straight-line distance to available drivers.

fields.label: Member Street Address
fields.description: Street address component of the member's stranded location (e.g., '123 Main St'). Displayed in SA detail views and the queue board to help dispatchers identify the call location.

fields.label: Member City
fields.description: City where the member is stranded. Part of the full address shown in detail views.

fields.label: Member State
fields.description: State where the member is stranded.

fields.label: Member Zip Code
fields.description: Postal/ZIP code of the member's stranded location.

fields.label: PTA — Promised Time of Arrival (Minutes)
fields.description: The number of minutes promised to the member at dispatch time — 'we'll have someone there in X minutes.' For Fleet: calculated by the FSL optimization engine based on driver distance and availability. For Towbook: entered by the Towbook dispatcher (often a rough estimate). Values of 0 mean 'field not populated' and 999+ mean 'no ETA given.' Both are sentinel values excluded from PTA accuracy calculations. Valid range: 1–998 minutes.

fields.label: PTA Due Timestamp
fields.description: The absolute timestamp when the driver was promised to arrive. Calculated as CreatedDate plus ERS_PTA__c minutes. Displayed in the Queue Board to show which calls are approaching or have passed their PTA deadline — helping dispatchers prioritize calls that are about to miss their promised time.

fields.label: Dispatch Method
fields.description: Identifies whether the call was handled by the internal fleet or an external contractor. 'Field Services' = internal fleet driver dispatched via the FSL optimization engine. 'Towbook' = external contractor dispatched via the Towbook system. This field is a Salesforce formula field set automatically at dispatch time. Determines the Fleet/Contractor badge shown on the garage dashboard and drives the dispatch mix pie chart.

fields.label: Auto-Assigned Flag
fields.description: True means the Service Appointment was automatically dispatched by the FSL optimization engine to the primary (first-choice) garage for the zone. False or null means it was manually dispatched, is a secondary/backup dispatch, or was sent via Towbook. Used to split acceptance rates into 'Primary' (auto-assigned, 1st choice) vs 'Secondary' (manual/backup) categories. Note: this field has known reliability issues and is cross-validated with SA History data.

fields.label: Parent (Spotted) Territory
fields.description: The zone/territory where the member is physically stranded — the 'spotted zone.' This is NOT the garage assigned to handle the call; it's the geographic zone where the member's car broke down. Combined with the Territory Priority Matrix to determine if the assigned garage is the primary (rank 1) or backup (rank 2+) for that zone. Drives the '1st Call %' and '2nd+ Call %' metrics.

fields.label: Facility Decline Reason
fields.description: If a garage declined this call, this field captures why — e.g., 'No Trucks Available', 'Out of Service Area', 'Driver Unavailable.' A non-null value means the garage declined the call. When a garage declines, the call cascades to the next garage in the zone chain, adding delay for the member. Used for acceptance rate calculations, decline analysis charts, and the decline rate in the composite scorer.

fields.label: Cancellation Reason
fields.description: If the call was cancelled, this field captures the reason. The key value is 'Member Could Not Wait%' — this means the member gave up waiting for the driver and cancelled the call themselves. A high 'Could Not Wait' rate is a direct indicator of member frustration and long response times. Other values like 'Duplicate Call', 'Problem Resolved' are shown in the cancellation breakdown chart.

fields.label: Spotting Number
fields.description: Legacy field that records which position this SA was in the dispatch sequence. A value of 1 means the call was sent to this garage first (1st call). A value greater than 1 means the call was cascaded from another garage (2nd+ call). Used as a fallback for 1st Call vs 2nd+ Call classification when SA History records are unavailable.

fields.label: Towbook Truck ID
fields.description: Identifier for the Towbook contractor's truck assigned to this call. Only populated for Towbook-dispatched calls (ERS_Dispatch_Method__c = 'Towbook'). Used internally to track which contractor truck handled the service.

fields.label: Towbook Driver Reference
fields.description: Links to the ServiceResource record for the Towbook contractor driver. Only populated for Towbook-dispatched calls. The related record (Off_Platform_Driver__r.Name) provides the contractor driver's display name.

fields.label: Dispatch Geolocation (Latitude)
fields.description: GPS latitude recorded at the time of dispatch. May differ from the member's reported location (Latitude field) if the dispatch location was adjusted or the member moved.

fields.label: Dispatch Geolocation (Longitude)
fields.description: GPS longitude recorded at the time of dispatch.

fields.label: Estimated Job Duration
fields.description: FSL managed package field estimating how many minutes the on-site service will take. Used by the scheduling engine for capacity planning and driver scheduling optimization.

fields.label: Schedule Mode
fields.description: FSL managed package field indicating how this SA was scheduled — automatic optimization, manual dispatch, or emergency override.

fields.label: Auto-Schedule Flag
fields.description: FSL managed package flag indicating whether this SA is eligible for automatic scheduling by the FSL optimization engine.

fields.label: Territory Record ID
fields.description: Unique Salesforce record ID for the garage/territory. Used as the primary key for all garage-level data grouping and API lookups.

fields.label: Garage Name
fields.description: Display name of the garage/territory (e.g., 'Tonawanda Towing', 'AAA Buffalo Fleet'). Shown in the garage list table, all page headers, map labels, and dropdown selectors throughout the app.

fields.label: Active Flag
fields.description: Whether this territory is currently active. Only active territories (IsActive = true) are shown in the app. Inactive territories are filtered out of all queries.

fields.label: Garage Location (Latitude)
fields.description: GPS latitude of the garage facility's physical location. Used to place the garage pin on the Command Center map and calculate distances from the garage to open calls.

fields.label: Garage Location (Longitude)
fields.description: GPS longitude of the garage facility.

fields.label: Garage Street Address
fields.description: Street address of the garage facility.

fields.label: Garage City
fields.description: City where the garage is located.

fields.label: Garage State
fields.description: State where the garage is located.

fields.label: Garage Zip Code
fields.description: Postal/ZIP code of the garage facility.

fields.label: Parent Territory
fields.description: If this territory is a sub-territory, this links to its parent. Used to establish territory hierarchy in the Salesforce FSL framework.

fields.label: Facility Account (Relationship)
fields.description: Links this garage territory to its Account record — the business entity of the garage. Through this relationship, the app accesses the garage's phone number (Account.Phone) and dispatch method classification (Account.Dispatch_Method__c).

fields.label: Work Type ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the work type record.

fields.label: Service Type Name
fields.description: The name of the service type: Tow, Battery, Tire, Lockout, Locksmith, Winch Out, Fuel / Miscellaneous, PVS, Jumpstart, or Tow Drop-Off. 'Tow Drop-Off' is the second leg of a tow — the truck dropping the car at the destination. It is excluded from ALL counts and response time calculations because the member's actual wait was measured on the Pick-Up SA. Work type also determines which driver skills are needed.

fields.label: Work Order ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the Work Order.

fields.label: Work Order Number
fields.description: Human-readable Work Order number (e.g., WO-00054321). This is the critical join key between calls and customer satisfaction surveys. When Qualtrics sends a survey, the response record (Survey_Result__c) stores this WO number as text — NOT a Salesforce lookup. The app matches surveys back to garages by joining on this number.

fields.label: WO Territory
fields.description: The territory/garage associated with this Work Order. Used to filter Work Orders by garage when querying survey data.

fields.label: WO Created Date
fields.description: When the Work Order was created. Used to sort and limit Work Order queries by date range.

fields.label: Driver/Truck ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the driver or truck resource.

fields.label: Driver / Truck Name
fields.description: Name of the driver or truck. For Fleet: the driver's actual name (e.g., 'John Smith'). For Towbook contractors: may be a truck identifier or company name. Displayed on the Driver Leaderboard showing per-driver performance metrics.

fields.label: Active Driver Flag
fields.description: Whether this driver/truck is currently active in the system. Only active resources are considered for dispatch recommendations and fleet size counts.

fields.label: Driver GPS (Latitude)
fields.description: Last known GPS latitude of the driver/truck. For Fleet: updated in real-time by the FSL mobile app as the driver moves. For Towbook: may not be available. Used to place driver pins on the Command Center map and calculate distance to open calls for dispatch recommendations.

fields.label: Driver GPS (Longitude)
fields.description: Last known GPS longitude of the driver/truck.

fields.label: GPS Last Updated
fields.description: Timestamp of when the driver's GPS position was last updated. Used to determine if a driver's location data is stale (e.g., if the FSL mobile app hasn't reported in hours, the location may be unreliable).

fields.label: Driver Type
fields.description: Classification of the driver — e.g., fleet driver, contractor, supervisor. Used to categorize resources and filter driver lists.

fields.label: Resource Type
fields.description: Standard FSL field classifying the resource as Technician, Dispatcher, Crew, etc. Helps differentiate between field resources and office staff.

fields.label: Driver Reference
fields.description: Links to the driver/truck (ServiceResource) who belongs to this territory. This is one half of the junction: 'which driver is assigned to which garage.'

fields.label: Territory Reference
fields.description: Links to the garage/territory (ServiceTerritory) where this driver is based. This is the other half of the junction: 'which garage does this driver belong to.'

fields.label: Membership Type
fields.description: Whether this driver is a Primary or Secondary/Relocation member of the territory. Primary members are the garage's main drivers. Secondary members may serve multiple territories. Affects fleet size calculations and dispatch priority.

fields.label: Skill ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the skill definition.

fields.label: Skill Name
fields.description: Human-readable name of the skill: Tow, Flat Bed, Battery, Tire, Lockout, Locksmith, Winch Out, Fuel, Jumpstart, PVS. Used to classify which service types a driver can handle. The scheduling engine matches call work type to required skills when recommending drivers.

fields.label: Driver Reference
fields.description: Links to the driver who has this skill.

fields.label: Skill Reference
fields.description: Links to the Skill that this driver possesses. Through this junction, the app determines: 'Driver A has skills [Tow, Battery, Tire]' and can match against the work type required for an open call.

fields.label: Assigned Driver
fields.description: The driver/truck assigned to a specific Service Appointment. Created when dispatch assigns a driver to a call. Used to build the Driver Leaderboard by linking individual calls to the driver who handled them.

fields.label: Assigned SA
fields.description: The Service Appointment this driver was assigned to. The other half of the junction linking a specific call to a specific driver.

fields.label: Parent SA Reference
fields.description: The Service Appointment this history record belongs to. Multiple history records per SA track each field change over time.

fields.label: Changed Field Name
fields.description: Which field was changed. Key values: 'ServiceTerritory' (territory assignment changed — used to track the sequence of garage assignments for 1st Call vs 2nd+ Call classification), 'Status' (lifecycle status changed — 'On Location' status change captures real Towbook driver arrival time).

fields.label: New Field Value
fields.description: The new value after the field change. For territory changes: the ID of the newly assigned garage (first NewValue in sequence = 1st call garage). For status changes: the new status value (look for 'On Location' to capture Towbook real arrival timestamp).

fields.label: Previous Field Value
fields.description: The previous value before the field change. Used to see which garage had the call before the current one.

fields.label: Change Timestamp
fields.description: When the field change occurred. For 'On Location' status changes on Towbook SAs, this timestamp IS the real driver arrival time — it's when the Towbook system reported the driver arrived at the member's location. More accurate than ActualStartTime for Towbook calls.

fields.label: Parent Zone (Spotted Territory)
fields.description: The zone/territory where members can be stranded — the geographic area. Each zone has one or more garages assigned to serve it, ordered by priority. This is the zone, NOT the garage. Think of it as 'Zone A is served by Garage 1 (priority 1), Garage 2 (priority 2), Garage 3 (priority 3).'

fields.label: Serving Garage
fields.description: The garage assigned to serve this zone at this priority level. For example, if Tonawanda Towing is Priority 1 for Zone A, this field points to Tonawanda's ServiceTerritory record.

fields.label: Priority Rank
fields.description: The priority ranking number for this garage in this zone. Priority 1 = first call (primary) — the system dispatches to this garage first. Priority 2 = second call (backup) — dispatched only if Priority 1 declines. Priority 3+ = further backups. Lower number = higher priority. A garage can be Priority 1 in some zones and Priority 2+ in others.

fields.label: Work Type Filter
fields.description: Which work types this priority assignment applies to (e.g., 'Tow', 'Battery/Light'). A garage may be the primary (Priority 1) for tow calls in a zone but secondary (Priority 2) for battery calls in the same zone. This allows work-type-specific cascade chains.

fields.label: Survey Record ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the survey result record.

fields.label: Overall Satisfaction Rating
fields.description: The member's overall satisfaction rating from the post-service Qualtrics survey. Values: 'Totally Satisfied', 'Satisfied', 'Neither Satisfied nor Dissatisfied', 'Dissatisfied', 'Totally Dissatisfied'. AAA accreditation target is 82% 'Totally Satisfied' rate. This drives the Customer Satisfaction dimension of the composite score (15% weight).

fields.label: Work Order Number (Survey Join Key)
fields.description: The Work Order Number this survey corresponds to, stored as text (NOT a Salesforce lookup). This is the join key that links surveys back to specific calls and garages. The app queries Work Orders for a garage, collects WO numbers, then finds matching surveys. Surveys can arrive days or weeks after the call.
usedIn: Satisfaction metrics (join key from surveys to garages)

fields.label: Facility Business Name
fields.description: Name of the garage's business entity/account. Accessed via ServiceTerritory.ERS_Facility_Account__r.Name.

fields.label: Garage Phone Number
fields.description: Main phone number for the garage facility. Displayed in the Command Center garage info panel so dispatchers can call the garage directly.

fields.label: Account Dispatch Method
fields.description: Account-level classification of how this garage primarily dispatches: internal fleet, Towbook contractor, or hybrid. Different from the per-SA ERS_Dispatch_Method__c which records how each individual call was dispatched. This is the garage's default/primary method.

fields.label: Polygon ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the polygon record.

fields.label: Polygon Name
fields.description: Name of the territory polygon, typically matching the territory name.

fields.label: Territory Reference
fields.description: Links this polygon boundary to its parent ServiceTerritory/garage. Multiple polygons can belong to one territory if the garage covers non-contiguous areas.

fields.label: Polygon Color
fields.description: Display color for the polygon boundary on the map (hex or color name).

fields.label: KML Boundary Data
fields.description: The geographic boundary definition in KML format. Contains the polygon coordinates that define the territory's coverage area. Parsed and rendered as a map overlay on the Command Center.

fields.label: Policy ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the scheduling policy.

fields.label: Policy Name
fields.description: Name of the scheduling policy (e.g., 'ERS Emergency Policy'). The FSL optimization engine uses this policy's rules and goals when auto-scheduling calls.

fields.label: Policy Last Modified
fields.description: When the scheduling policy was last updated. Used to detect if scheduling rules have changed.

fields.label: Related Work Type
fields.description: Links to the WorkType that requires this skill. For example, the 'Tow' WorkType has a SkillRequirement pointing to it, requiring drivers to have the 'Tow' skill.

fields.label: Required Skill
fields.description: Links to the Skill record that is required. Together with RelatedRecordId, this row says 'work type X requires skill Y.'

fields.label: Skill Name (via relationship)
fields.description: The human-readable name of the required skill (e.g., 'Tow', 'Battery', 'Flat Bed'). Accessed through the Skill relationship rather than a direct field.

fields.label: Truck Name/Number
fields.description: Display name or unit number of the truck (e.g., 'Truck 101', 'Unit FB-3'). Shown in the Command Center map and driver detail views.

fields.label: Assigned Driver
fields.description: The driver currently assigned to this truck. Only trucks with a non-null driver are considered active and available for dispatch. Links the physical vehicle to the driver operating it.

fields.label: Truck Capabilities
fields.description: What types of service this truck can perform — tow, flatbed, light service, battery, etc. Used alongside driver skills to determine if a truck+driver combo can handle a specific call type.

fields.label: Legacy Truck ID
fields.description: Legacy identifier for the truck from the previous dispatch system. Used for cross-referencing with historical data and external systems.

fields.label: Record Type
fields.description: Filtered to 'ERS Truck' to select only roadside assistance vehicles from the Asset table. Other record types (general assets, equipment) are excluded.

fields.label: Shift Record ID
fields.description: Unique Salesforce record ID for the shift.

fields.label: Driver
fields.description: The driver who is working this shift. Links the shift time window to a specific driver.

fields.label: Shift Start
fields.description: When the driver's shift begins. The driver is considered available for dispatch only during the window between StartTime and EndTime.

fields.label: Shift End
fields.description: When the driver's shift ends.

fields.label: Shift Status
fields.description: Current status of the shift (e.g., Confirmed, Tentative, Published). Determines if the shift counts toward available capacity.

fields.label: Shift Territory
fields.description: Which garage/territory this shift is assigned to. A driver may have shifts at different garages on different days.

fields.label: Absence Record ID
fields.description: Unique Salesforce record ID for the absence.

fields.label: Absent Driver
fields.description: The driver who is absent. Any overlapping absence makes a driver unavailable for dispatch.

fields.label: Absence Start
fields.description: When the absence begins.

fields.label: Absence End
fields.description: When the absence ends. The driver becomes available again after this time.

fields.label: Absence Type
fields.description: Reason for the absence — vacation, sick leave, training, truck maintenance, personal, etc.

fields.label: Territory
fields.description: Which garage/territory this PTA configuration applies to.

fields.label: Work Type
fields.description: Which service type this PTA target applies to (Tow, Battery, Tire, etc.). Different work types have different PTA targets because service complexity varies.

fields.label: PTA Minutes Target
fields.description: The configured PTA (minutes to promise the member) for this territory + work type combination. The PTA Advisor compares this target against actual response times to identify garages that are over- or under-promising.

fields.label: Parent Policy
fields.description: The scheduling policy this goal belongs to.

fields.label: Goal Weight
fields.description: How heavily this goal is weighted in the optimization. Higher weight means the FSL engine prioritizes this goal over lower-weighted ones when making trade-off decisions.

fields.label: Service Goal Reference
fields.description: The service goal (optimization objective) this policy goal links to.

fields.label: Goal ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the service goal.

fields.label: Goal Name
fields.description: Name of the optimization goal (e.g., 'Minimize Travel', 'ASAP', 'Resource Priority').

fields.label: Parent Policy
fields.description: The scheduling policy this work rule assignment belongs to.

fields.label: Work Rule Reference
fields.description: The work rule (constraint) applied under this policy.

fields.label: Rule ID
fields.description: Unique ID for the work rule.

fields.label: Rule Name
fields.description: Name of the constraint rule (e.g., 'Match Skills', 'Match Territory', 'Working Hours', 'Maximum Travel Distance'). These rules prevent invalid dispatch assignments.
